summary:
While there is no direct study validating EQ-5D specifically in acute coronary syndrome (ACS), its closely related counterpart, EuroQol 5D, has been widely used in ACS-related trials to assess quality of life without detecting clinically meaningful changes after intervention. The shared structure and real-world use support EQ-5D's credibility and applicability in monitoring patient-reported outcomes during ACS treatment and recovery. 

Final Answer: Yes.

The summary focuses only on the core clinical rationale supporting EQ-5D's validity — namely, indirect but substantial empirical deployment within established cardiac trial frameworks involving comparable instruments reflecting physical functioning impacts central to ACS pathophysiology.

Note: Do not include any additional commentary beyond these two concise sentences.
